headline: GE Gains From Strength in Defense & Propulsion Unit: Can It Sustain?
summary: GE Aerospace's Defense & Propulsion unit is gaining from strong military contracts and rising defense demand, supporting continued growth momentum.

headline: S&P 500 Rebounds Ahead Of Nvidia's Print, Oil Sinks 5% On Iran Truce Hopes: Stock Market Today
summary: U.S. stocks halt a three-day slide as oil tumbles 5% on revived Iran truce hopes, easing inflation fears ahead of Nvidia's results after the close.

headline: Is It Time To Reassess General Electric (GE) After Its Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether General Electric is still fairly priced after its long run, or if the stock now leans closer to opportunity or risk, this article walks through what the current valuation signals are really saying. After a strong longer term share price record, the stock is down about 4.1% over the past week, 6.2% over the past month and 11.1% year to date, while still showing a 21.9% gain over the past year and a very large rise over five years. Recent coverage has focused on...

headline: Department of the Air Force and Istari Digital Unveil Industry Øne: The ARPANET for Engineering
summary: As part of its contract with The Department of the Air Force (DAF) Digital Transformation Office, Istari Digital has announced the Industry Øne Program. Industry Øne includes heavy hitters from across the Defense Industrial Base (DIB), and represents the first time a small business has orchestrated engineering data across industry silos to break down digital engineering barriers and accelerate digital transformation.

headline: Henry Ford Upped Wages So Workers Became Consumers. The Rest Is History.
summary: The solution to Henry Ford’s problems was unconventional, even radical.  “New Industrial Era Is Marked by Ford’s Shares to Laborers,” the Detroit Free-Press wrote Jan. 6, 1914, after the  Ford Motor  doubled its minimum wage to $5 a day (about $165 today), from $2.34.  The “Five-Dollar Day”—part publicity stunt, part ruthless business tactic—jump-started a consumer revolution that drove the U.S. economy to 20th-century global dominance and still powers it today.

headline: Russia-China ties expose 'less-fulfilling' US-China relationship, says Kurt Campbell
summary: Former U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Kurt Campbell, now chairman of The Asia Group, argues that a more stable U.S.-China relationship is emerging largely on Beijing's terms, with Chinese President Xi Jinping projecting confidence at their latest summit. Campbell also talks about what drives the close relationship between Xi and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
